---
title: "Week 4: Feb 16 - Feb 22, 2026"
subtitle: "Unit II: JavaScript Advanced — Scope, Objects & DOM"
---

## Week Overview

This week advances your JavaScript skills with block-scoped variables, objects and object literals, DOM manipulation, array methods, and a practical revision session.

**Course Outcome:** CO-2 (K3 - Application)  
**Duration:** 1 week (4 lectures + 1 lab)  
**Lab:** JavaScript Functions & Events

---

## Lecture 13: Block Scope Variables (let, const) {#lecture-13}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
[{{< fa list-check >}} Take Quiz](/quiz/lecture-13-quiz.qmd)
[{{< fa desktop >}} View Lecture](/lectures/lecture-13.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
```{=html}
<div class="ratio ratio-16x9 mb-3">
  <iframe class="lecture-preview-frame" src="/lectures/lecture-13.html" title="Lecture presentation preview" loading="lazy" style="border: 1px solid #343a40;" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```
[{{< fa flask >}} Open Demo](/html/demos/lecture-13-demo.html)
[{{< fa code >}} Demo JS (app.js)](/html/demos/lecture-13/app.js)
:::


### var vs let vs const
```javascript
var x = 1;    // function-scoped, can be redeclared (avoid in modern JS)
let y = 2;    // block-scoped, reassignable
const z = 3;  // block-scoped, cannot be reassigned
```

### Block Scope in Action
```javascript
if (true) {
  let blockVar   = 10;   // only accessible inside this block
  const blockConst = 20;
  console.log(blockVar); // 10 ✅
}
console.log(blockVar);   // ReferenceError ❌
```

### Template Literals (ES6)
```javascript
const name = "Alice";
const age  = 25;
console.log(`Name: ${name}, Age: ${age}`);  // Name: Alice, Age: 25
```

### Destructuring & Spread
```javascript
// Array destructuring
const [a, b] = [10, 20];

// Object destructuring
const { x, y } = { x: 1, y: 2 };

// Spread operator
const arr1 = [1, 2, 3];
const arr2 = [...arr1, 4, 5]; // [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
```

---

## Lecture 14: Objects & Object Literals in JavaScript {#lecture-14}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
[{{< fa list-check >}} Take Quiz](/quiz/lecture-14-quiz.qmd)
[{{< fa desktop >}} View Lecture](/lectures/lecture-14.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
```{=html}
<div class="ratio ratio-16x9 mb-3">
  <iframe class="lecture-preview-frame" src="/lectures/lecture-14.html" title="Lecture presentation preview" loading="lazy" style="border: 1px solid #343a40;" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```
:::


### Object Literal Syntax
```javascript
const student = {
  name:   "Riya",
  roll:   42,
  branch: "MCA",
  greet: function() { return `Hello, I am ${this.name}`; }
};
```

### Accessing & Modifying Properties
```javascript
console.log(student.name);       // dot notation
console.log(student['branch']);  // bracket notation
student.city = "Noida";          // add new property
delete student.roll;             // delete a property
```

### Nested Objects
```javascript
const course = {
  title: "Web Technology",
  instructor: { name: "Mr. Nag", dept: "MCA" }
};
console.log(course.instructor.name);  // Mr. Nag
```

### `this` Keyword
```javascript
const obj = {
  value: 100,
  show: function() { console.log(this.value); }
};
obj.show(); // 100
```

---

## Lecture 15: DOM Manipulation & Array Methods {#lecture-15}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
[{{< fa list-check >}} Take Quiz](/quiz/lecture-15-quiz.qmd)
[{{< fa desktop >}} View Lecture](/lectures/lecture-15.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
```{=html}
<div class="ratio ratio-16x9 mb-3">
  <iframe class="lecture-preview-frame" src="/lectures/lecture-15.html" title="Lecture presentation preview" loading="lazy" style="border: 1px solid #343a40;" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```
:::


### DOM Selection
```javascript
document.getElementById('id');           // by id
document.querySelector('.class');        // first match (CSS selector)
document.querySelectorAll('p');          // all matches → NodeList
```

### DOM Manipulation
```javascript
element.textContent = "New text";        // set text
element.innerHTML   = "<b>Bold</b>";     // set HTML
element.classList.add('active');         // add class
element.classList.toggle('hidden');      // toggle class
element.style.color = 'red';             // inline style
element.setAttribute('href', '/path');   // set attribute
```

### Creating & Removing Elements
```javascript
const li = document.createElement('li');
li.textContent = "New item";
document.querySelector('ul').appendChild(li);
li.remove(); // remove from DOM
```

### Array Methods
```javascript
const nums = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
nums.map(x => x * 2);               // [2, 4, 6, 8, 10]
nums.filter(x => x > 2);            // [3, 4, 5]
nums.reduce((sum, x) => sum + x, 0); // 15
nums.find(x => x > 3);              // 4
nums.includes(3);                   // true
nums.slice(1, 3);                   // [2, 3]
```

---

## Lecture 16: Revision & Practical (JavaScript + DOM) {#lecture-16}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
[{{< fa list-check >}} Take Quiz](/quiz/lecture-16-quiz.qmd)
[{{< fa desktop >}} View Lecture](/lectures/lecture-16.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
```{=html}
<div class="ratio ratio-16x9 mb-3">
  <iframe class="lecture-preview-frame" src="/lectures/lecture-16.html" title="Lecture presentation preview" loading="lazy" style="border: 1px solid #343a40;" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```
:::


### Interactive Task List (Combining It All)
```javascript
const input = document.querySelector('#taskInput');
const list  = document.querySelector('#taskList');

document.querySelector('#addBtn').addEventListener('click', () => {
  if (!input.value.trim()) return;
  const li = document.createElement('li');
  li.textContent = input.value;
  li.addEventListener('click', () => li.classList.toggle('done'));
  list.appendChild(li);
  input.value = '';
});
```

### Revision Checklist
- [ ] Declare variables correctly with `const` / `let`
- [ ] Write and call functions including arrow functions
- [ ] Attach event listeners and use the event object
- [ ] Write `if/else`, `switch`, `for`, and `while` correctly
- [ ] Create, modify, and delete DOM elements
- [ ] Use `map`, `filter`, `reduce` on arrays
- [ ] Understand block scope vs function scope

---

## Lab 4: JavaScript Array Methods and Nested Functions

### Objectives
- Apply array methods (`map`, `filter`, `reduce`) to real data
- Practice nested functions and closures
- Combine DOM manipulation with event handling

### Activities
1. Create an array of student objects and use `map` to render them in a table
2. Add a search input that `filter`s the rendered list in real time
3. Use `reduce` to calculate a total (e.g., average marks)
4. Implement a counter using closures
5. Commit all work to GitHub

### Deliverables
- HTML + JS file with array methods and DOM updates
- Working search/filter functionality
- GitHub commit with changes

---

## Key Takeaways

✅ `const` / `let` are block-scoped; prefer them over `var`  
✅ Objects group related data and behaviour in key-value pairs  
✅ DOM manipulation lets JavaScript change live HTML content  
✅ Array methods (`map`, `filter`, `reduce`) enable concise data processing  
✅ Combine scope, objects, DOM, and events to build interactive UI components

---

## Next Week

Unit III: Java Servlet Programming (Server-Side Web):
- Lecture 17: Servlet Overview & Architecture
- Lecture 18: Servlet Interface & Life Cycle
- Lecture 19: Handling HTTP GET & POST Requests
- Lecture 20: Redirecting Requests & Request Dispatching
